title: Phase 42 — paymentService URL validation + appleAuth type safety

stories.title: paymentService: validate URL from getPortalUrl()
stories.description:
services/paymentService.ts has a single function getPortalUrl() that returns data.url without any validation. If the backend returns a malformed response, data.url would be undefined and crash when calling code tries to open it.

Add validation:
- Check data?.url exists
- Check it's a string
- Check it starts with 'https://'
- Throw an Error if any check fails
acceptance: data.url validated for existence, type, and https scheme before returning
Throws Error with descriptive message if invalid

stories.title: appleAuth: add type safety and try/catch
stories.description:
services/appleAuth.ts has signInWithApple() that:
1. Calls api.post('/auth/apple') without a generic type parameter — returns implicit any response
2. Has no try/catch — network errors or 500s will propagate as unhandled
3. Returns Promise<void> instead of Promise<AuthResponse>

Fix:
- Import AuthResponse from types/auth (already imported in api.ts)
- Change api.post('/auth/apple', ...) to api.post<AuthResponse>('/auth/apple', ...)
- Destructure: const { data } = await api.post<AuthResponse>(...)
- Change return type to Promise<AuthResponse> and return data
- Remove the comment '// Backend may not have Apple auth yet' since it's already handled in LoginScreen
acceptance: data.token and data.refreshToken accessed via typed destructuring
